title: Enchanting in Beta Minecraft
description: It brings some enchantments from newer Minecraft, to the diamond sword tools, and it gives them buffs. The crafting recipes are actually cheap because the buffs are overpowered on the enchanted tools.
body:
You probably play the new minecraft versions because you care about enchantmens, right? Well, I saw the same problem, and decided to fix it. In this mod, in the current moment, there are 5 enchanted tools. The enchanting does not work like in newer Minecraft, where you place some bookshelfs, and then an enchanting table, and other things, because stationapi does not provide that much block creation enhancements, so you have to stick with this idea. The enchanted tools can be obtained really easily, i made the crafting recipe easy as hell because, even myself, hate seeing complicated recipes in modpacks. Also, the tools contain the maxed out enchantmens from newer Minecraft versions, as Sharpness V, and Efficiency V. I can add Fire aspect II in a new version of the mod, but for now this is the initial release. Here is the crafting recipe for the Enchanted Diamond Sword:
![Crafting recipe for Enchanted Diamond Sword](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e7f50538499d88a98e18f40bc7a53543c6fdac42.jpeg)

Simple, right? And you get a decent weapon, one shot every animal, you see, even mobs.

**Instalation guide**:
Download the "enchanted_beta_mc-1.0.0.jar" file, see the required StationAPI jar file as well, and the optional ones, if the game asks for them.
Download, for example, Prism Launcher, or Multimc, and drag and drop the mods in the instance's folder in the folder called "mods". And you're good to go.
